seoTitle: Fusemi (Senwei) Technical Support | IGBT, MOSFET, SiC Selection Guides
seoDescription: Access Fusemi technical support resources including IGBT module selection guides, MOSFET application notes, and SiC device guidance.

faqs.question: How do I get technical support for Fusemi products?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support: 1) Direct FAE support for device selection and application design; 2) Technical documentation including datasheets and application notes; 3) Email support with 24-hour response; 4) Phone support for urgent issues; 5) On-site support for large projects.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for device selection and application support.

faqs.question: What technical resources are available?
faqs.answer: Available resources: 1) Datasheets with electrical characteristics; 2) Selection guides for different applications; 3) Application notes for specific topologies; 4) Thermal design guidelines; 5) Gate drive recommendations; 6) Protection design guides.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our technical library or contact our FAE for specific resources.

articles.title: IGBT Module Selection Guide
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide for selecting IGBT modules based on voltage, current, switching frequency, and thermal requirements.

contentSections.heading: Introduction
contentSections.content: IGBT module selection is critical for reliable power electronics design. This guide covers key parameters and selection criteria.

contentSections.heading: Voltage Rating Selection
contentSections.content: Select IGBT voltage rating 2-3x maximum DC link voltage. For 400V AC systems, use 1200V IGBT. For 690V AC, use 1700V IGBT.

contentSections.heading: Current Rating Sizing
contentSections.content: Size current rating with 1.5-2x margin for overload capability. Consider RMS current, peak current, and thermal limitations.

faeInsights.insight: Always verify thermal performance under worst-case conditions. Short-circuit withstand is critical for motor drive applications.
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this IGBT Module Selection Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

faqs.question: What is VCE(sat)?
faqs.answer: [Data Pending] Answer to be verified with manufacturer technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compare VCE(sat) for conduction loss calculation.

articles.title: Power MOSFET Selection Guide
articles.summary: Guide for selecting power MOSFETs based on voltage, RDS(on), switching characteristics, and thermal requirements.

contentSections.heading: Introduction
contentSections.content: MOSFET selection balances conduction losses, switching losses, and cost. This guide provides selection criteria.

contentSections.heading: RDS(on) Selection
contentSections.content: Lower RDS(on) reduces conduction losses but increases cost and gate charge. Select based on efficiency requirements and thermal budget.

faeInsights.insight: RDS(on) doubles from 25°C to 125°C. Always calculate losses at operating temperature.
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this Power MOSFET Selection Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

faqs.question: What is Qg?
faqs.answer: [Data Pending] Answer to be verified with manufacturer technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select lower Qg for high-frequency applications.

articles.title: SiC Device Selection Guide
articles.summary: Guide for selecting SiC MOSFETs and diodes for high-efficiency power conversion applications.

contentSections.heading: Introduction
contentSections.content: SiC devices offer significant advantages over silicon for high-voltage, high-frequency, and high-temperature applications.

contentSections.heading: Gate Drive Requirements
contentSections.content: SiC MOSFETs typically require 0V/18V or -3V/18V gate drive. Use dedicated SiC gate drivers for optimal performance.

faeInsights.insight: SiC enables higher efficiency and reduced cooling. Gate drive design is critical for optimal performance.
faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this SiC Device Selection Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

faqs.question: What gate voltage for SiC MOSFET?
faqs.answer: [Data Pending] Answer to be verified with manufacturer technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use recommended gate voltage from datasheet.

articles.title: Thermal Design Guide for Power Semiconductors
articles.summary: Comprehensive thermal design guide for power semiconductors including IGBT modules and MOSFETs. Covers heat sink selection, thermal interface materials, and thermal modeling.
content: Thermal design is critical for reliable operation of power semiconductors. This guide covers key considerations for effective thermal management.
Heat sink selection involves calculating thermal resistance requirements. Determine maximum allowable junction temperature, ambient temperature, and device power dissipation.
Thermal interface materials (TIM) fill microscopic air gaps between device and heat sink. Select TIM based on thermal conductivity, thickness, and long-term reliability.
Thermal modeling using software tools can predict operating temperatures before building prototypes. Validate models with actual measurements under worst-case conditions.

faeInsights.insight: The most common thermal design mistake is insufficient margin. Always design for worst-case conditions including maximum ambient temperature and degraded TIM performance over time.
faeInsights.logic: Thermal design process: Calculate power dissipation → Determine thermal resistance budget → Select heat sink → Choose TIM → Validate with testing.
keyTakeaways: Design for worst-case conditions with margin
TIM selection is as important as heat sink
commonPitfalls: Poor mounting pressure affecting TIM performance
bestPractices: Select TIM with proven long-term stability

customerCases.challenge: IGBT modules overheating in compact enclosure with limited airflow.
customerCases.solution: Redesigned thermal system with optimized heat sink and high-performance TIM. Added thermal modeling for validation.
customerCases.result: Junction temperature reduced by 25°C. System reliability improved from 95% to 99.5%.

faqs.question: How do I calculate required heat sink thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Calculate required heat sink thermal resistance using: Rth_hs = (Tj_max - Ta) / Pd - Rth_jc - Rth_tim - Rth_cs. Where Tj_max is maximum junction temperature, Ta is ambient temperature, Pd is power dissipation, and Rth terms are thermal resistances of junction-to-case, TIM, and case-to-sink. Example: For Tj_max=150°C, Ta=50°C, Pd=500W, Rth_jc=0.1K/W, Rth_tim=0.2K/W: Rth_hs = (150-50)/500 - 0.1 - 0.2 = 0.2 - 0.3 = -0.1. Negative result indicates need for lower Rth_jc device or better cooling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate thermal resistance budget. Select heat sink with margin. Contact FAE for complex thermal designs.
